songs.title: Monthly
songs.note: Yes, this track started, in same ways, in Suno. But it's not a Suno track. I'll post this on my Suno site at https://null.dangerthirdrail.com/#monthly/ as well, but I think it works standalone.

songs.title: Trash on the Nash (tional Mall)
songs.note: I'm not even gonna lie, the movie A Complete Unknown has given me a renewed interest in folk music.

songs.title: CHP-1907
songs.note: Sometimes it's nice to just work within the "restrictions" of a "chiptune".

songs.title: 'ELE-4951'
songs.note: Another untitled electronic track.

songs.title: Skinny Jeans
songs.note: Sigh. But at least I'm making music again.

songs.title: Great Things
songs.note: The cool part is that I wrote this rather melancholy piece very quickly. The less cool part is that I phoned in the vocal performance and it's pretty mediocre.

songs.title: Star Star
songs.note: When I went to name this, I almost called it “Star Child”. I’m pretty sure David Bowie has a copyright to that (yes I know, you can’t copyright titles). Either way, that seemed too trite, so I went with the altogether sillier Star Star.

songs.title: The Moon Rises The Moon
songs.note: I recently watched _The Devil and Daniel Johnston_ and it inspired me to just go for it a bit and put some of my goofier song ideas out there.

songs.title: Goodbyes
songs.note: A two minute pessimistic pop punk song with synth. What more do you want from me?

songs.title: What Will It Take
songs.note: For once I went back to a song that I started earlier this month and I'm glad I did. I already had the vocal melody, but I added the lyrics and recorded the vocals.

songs.title: 'ELE-1005'
songs.note: Didn't feel like coming up with a "clever name" for this electronic composition, but I feel it's one of the best I've done in years.

songs.title: Just For A Minute
songs.note: I saw that __Strawberry Fields Forever__ was recorded in 55 hours. This was recorded in 1. Yes, it shows, but I like the raw unpolished energy and sound here.

songs.title: Mirrored Space
songs.note: Maybe the best part about this weird piece is when it fades out? :P

songs.title: You Don't Care
songs.note: I think this song kind of embodies the 'formula' I've established for myself with songs like _Failure_ on my last EP. It's not groundbreaking, but I don't particularly care (see what I did there). I actually like the lyric, vocal melody and synth parts quite a bit.

songs.title: Beautiful Thing
songs.note: I think this song is pretty. It's nice. Though the topic matter is a bit depressing. Still, I was going to add drums and bass but I think it's rather pleasant the way it is.

songs.title: Phi
songs.note: This was so obviously a hip hop beat from the start. I don't rap, and I don't know anyone who raps, so I took a vocal sample from [LibriVox](https://librivox.org) to fill it out a bit.

songs.title: Curious Metaphor
songs.note: I composed this while traveling on a bus back from Tahoe. It is my second composition on the OP-1 and my first "real" feeling one, like it stands on its own and is basically finished.

songs.title: Are You Ready Now
songs.note: This is definitely not done. But it's also March and I haven't uploaded a proper song yet this year, and this is the closest I have to one. Probably needs bass and some kind of synth/lead line.

songs.title: Bubbles
songs.note: At the start of this year my goal was to write a song a day. I'm not sure if I've even averaged a song a week, to be honest. I'm still happy with my output, but hopefully I can do more in 2019.

songs.title: Dragged
songs.note: The first full track I've made with my new bass is this prog/jazz groove. I used the Ableton Probability Pack on the drums, with just a basic kick and snare backing it. The title is just from the feel I get from the song.

songs.title: Eyes Open Wide
songs.note: I was actually trying to write some cool chords for possibly a dance track, but these were too jazzy for me to leave to something like that, so I wrote a little mini jazz tune.

songs.title: Do Don't
songs.note: I wanted to try and write a pop or pop rock song with a really catchy chorus, and I think I largely succeeded. I'm especially happy with the background vocals, they were really fun to record.

songs.title: Cradle
songs.note: I liked the bass sounds and texture I was getting with this, it was a nice little groove. I didn't feel like making it into a "slammin dance track" so instead I took it in a jazzy direction.

songs.title: PSA
songs.note: I took the [previous song](/psi) and added some clips from a podcast that I found on the Free Music Archive. I can only assume they mean free as in freedom and I have the rights to do this.

songs.title: psi
songs.note: This is an ambient track in the truest sense of the word. It would probably sound good with some spoken word stuff put over the top of it, maybe I can find something like that in the Internet Archive or something.

songs.title: Fizzle
songs.note: I made this as a 4 bar loop, which it was great as, but then when I thought of exporting it and presenting it as such, I realized I should just take the extra effort to progressively add and remove some elements. Not really a finished product, just a demo.

songs.title: Sub
songs.note: Okay, now I'm hitting an instrumental track groove, definitely. Listened to some dub today, and the influence is pretty clear.

songs.title: Four Sided Triangle
songs.note: Similar to last night's experiment with sounds, just trying to explore in freeform and see what comes out.

songs.title: Flywheel
songs.note: I was just playing around with some different sounds and came across this, which seems relatively pleasing, if simple.

songs.title: Dusted
songs.note: I was originally thinking of this chord progression and the downtempo rhodes as kind of a rock ballad of sorts. But I never wrote any words, and it kind of evolved into a pretty groovy electronic piece. This is the kind of electronica that I like making, honestly, not too genre specific but with a good groove.

songs.title: Wipe
songs.note: I picked the first drum track I saw, which gives this pop rock track a really weird feel as far as drums go. No synth either, which is rare for me (I have a guitar part that does a little lead fill).

songs.title: Radio Machine mkII
songs.note: I've had this song for a while now, and the original demo of just the verse and chorus is also posted on this site. But I've wanted to add a fuller arrangement and a bridge and I hadn't gotten around to it. Now I can say it's definitely finished.

songs.title: RAP-2550
songs.note: Everything I make recently is like a half-hearted hip hop beat.

songs.title: Alarm
songs.note: I tried this funky chord and I wound up arpeggiating it a bit too much, so now it sounds like an alarm on a phone.

songs.title: Scream Into the Void
songs.note: This track deserves a better description than this.

songs.title: Failure
songs.note: Synth rock about failure. How many times did I fail while making this track?

songs.title: Space Addict
songs.note: Broke out of my 6 week song writing drought with this weird glitch/noise piece.

songs.title: Monday Song
songs.note:
Case of the Mondays? It's amazing how few lyrics a 2 minute punk song has.

Follow my progress writing a song a day in 2018 at https://www.songaday2018.com

songs.title: Secret World
songs.note: Just a goofy pop punk track.

songs.title: Ship Has Sailed
songs.note: Just trying to get some lyrics down on paper here.

songs.title: Painball
songs.note: Real writing from deep down, seriously.

songs.title: City
songs.note: This is all I had in me today, maybe I'll come back and expand it tomorrow before working on the next song.

songs.title: Grind
songs.note: Actually this topic was really on my mind.

songs.title: Waste Your Life
songs.note: Sticking with "quick" demos for song a day: same drum track, no bass part, quick and dirty vocals, etc.

songs.title: New Year
songs.note:
My first track for Song a Day in 2018. https://www.songaday2018.com

Remember, I'm writing a song "for" each day in 2018, not "on" each day in 2018.

songs.title: Tavern
songs.note: Just a chill little melody, sounds to me like music for a tavern in an RPG.

songs.title: Ride It Out
songs.note: Bluesy and Grungey

songs.title: Forgetting
songs.note: Feel like this could be part of a movie soundtrack, like when the super spies are trying to hack the bank computer or something.

songs.title: RAP-3648
songs.note: Something that I came up with, just messing around. Let me know if you're interested in rapping over this!

songs.title: RAP-6566
songs.note: First track with Reason 10!

songs.title: Drove Her Crazy
songs.note: This track apparently drove her crazy...

songs.title: Waiting By The Phone
songs.note: Part of the synth sound is a bit too familiar for me, but I decided to roll with it.

songs.title: Elemental
songs.note: I wanted to make a track that would be deserving of a cool animated music video and this is what I came up with.

songs.title: Dream Girl mkII
songs.note:
So go listen to https://soundcloud.com/travis-briggs/dream-girl

I'll wait.

Yes, it's in fact just the post-chorus of this song. I thought I had posted the whole song when in fact I had exported only the post chorus.

#facepalm

songs.title: You're A Star
songs.note: The chorus to this one occurred to me fully formed, and I think I did a good job fleshing it out. I like the lyrics and melody more than the arrangement/production, which is very rough.

songs.title: Coffee
songs.note: I've stopped putting any effort whatsoever into the drums because I don't feel like it's really worth it. If the song is something I want to publish for real, I know a person in the UK that can do the drums...

songs.title: At Least I've Tried
songs.note: Meta song talking about staying inside and writing songs on a beautiful Saturday.

songs.title: Power Chords
songs.note: A little self deprecation for a Saturday afternoon.

songs.title: Submarine
songs.note: I really wish I had a three.js 3D or other 2D digital artwork to attach to this one.

songs.title: Vom
songs.note: Something I threw together quickly using mostly Serum and Ableton

songs.title: Dream Girl
songs.note: I went through a very particular method I saw online to write the melody for this track, but I'm not sure it worked out that well.

songs.title: The Dance
songs.note: Song a week, week ?. A 90 second punk rock number.

songs.title: Spy
songs.note:
Song-a-week challenge, week #3.

https://www.reddit.com/r/songaweek/comments/5os6ia/submissions_week_3_theme_seventh/

songs.title: Hometown
songs.note:
Posted as part of Reddit's songaweek challenge: https://www.reddit.com/r/songaweek/

Here's to 52 songs in 2017!

songs.title: Radio Machine
songs.note: Quirky, old-timey sounding song. Early demo version, very different from the finished track that wound up on the Radio Machine EP.

songs.title: Boredom
songs.note: What can I say, I was bored!

songs.title: Steam Room
songs.note: Kind of a dark, uptempo number

songs.title: You're Gonna Die Alone (Like Me)
songs.note: I wrote this song over five years ago, but for some reason never recorded it. This is the uptempo version, usually I play this acoustic.

songs.title: Problem With You
songs.note: How Abby and I feel about the Mets right now

songs.title: Raspberry Champagne
songs.note: Another instrumental. Abby came up with the title.

songs.title: With Feeling
songs.note: An instrumental with some guitars and some synth.

songs.title: Lucifer
songs.note: This one is pretty different from most tracks I make, besides the past two.

songs.title: Rainbow
songs.note: Basically the same song as the last track I uploaded.

songs.title: Stacked
songs.note: A quick thing I whipped together while practicing my minor pentatonic scale.

songs.title: Break Some Shit
songs.note: I basically wanted to start a song with a pad and see where it took me. Quite pleased with the result

songs.title: The Ghost
songs.note: I mastered this one with LANDR, go figure.

songs.title: Everything But You
songs.note: Song I wrote that is actually more optimistic than it seems.

songs.title: Getting There
songs.note: Another simple chiptune, not sure what the inspiration for this one was.

songs.title: 'ELE-6291'
songs.note: Quick demo of a simple electronic beat.

songs.title: Cold And Cruel
songs.note: More songs about existentialist dread, yay!

songs.title: Every Day
songs.note: Not a masterpiece, but a nice little demo.

songs.title: Operator Zero
songs.note: Not the most complex chiptune out there, but it works.

songs.title: In My Head
songs.note: Something that I whipped up on this hot Sunday.

songs.title: CA Layers
songs.note: Soundtrack to the 3D animation at [http://gallery.travisbriggs.com/ca-layers/](http://gallery.travisbriggs.com/3d/ca-layers/)

songs.title: Drip
songs.note: Background music for [http://gallery.travisbriggs.com/3d/drip/](http://gallery.travisbriggs.com/3d/drip/)

songs.title: Cube Box
songs.note: Music to accompany [https://gallery.travisbriggs.com/3d/cube-box.html](https://gallery.travisbriggs.com/3d/cube-box.html)

songs.title: Just Havin Fun
songs.note: Trying to get back into the swing of recording, trying a setup where I record acoustic guitar and vocals at the same time. Feels good!

songs.title: CRT - 7100
songs.note: This is just a sketch, but it's good to get some music out there again.

songs.title: Already Dead
songs.note: This is pretty much what I'm thinking of when I'm talking about electro-punk.

songs.title: Bedroom Song
songs.note: Made this track by "cutting up" a sample of me playing the guitar in Ableton. Came out kind of interesting.

songs.title: Melt
songs.note: Abby wrote the lyrics for this one. We put them "in a hat" (Ruby script) and picked them randomly.

songs.title: Control Shift
songs.note: I'm not sure if this is really dubstep so don't get mad at me. I was just messing around with distorted guitar and odd effects, and of course wobbly bass...

songs.title: We Sang
songs.note: Got the melodica out for this one. Melodica solo!

songs.title: Yellow Ones
songs.note: SImple chiptune based on some chords I found lying around.

songs.title: Exploration
songs.note: A very simple little chiptune, I can't imagine one that would be more simple!

songs.title: Game Music 1703
songs.note: I had an idea for an organ sound fading into a space-y synth, and this is the little clip I built around it.

songs.title: So Lonely
songs.note: I am very proud of this song's thick harmony, even on the guitar (where I learned some sus and add chords recently). I had a lot of fun recording the layered vocal tracks.

songs.title: You're Safe Here
songs.note: My voice is not suited for this style of singing. Also if you write a melody on piano it's not always singable. But I really like this song as a concept.

songs.title: Ashes And Dust
songs.note: I tried to tell a complete story with a beginning, middle and end. But it took me 9 minutes. Good luck finishing this one...

songs.title: Go Home
songs.note: Clearly not my best work, and not even fully written

songs.title: Don't Wanna Go
songs.note:
Week 1 of the Song a Week challenge (http://www.reddit.com/r/songaweek)

My first attempt at an "ambient" intro. I also layered some guitars in this one.

songs.title: Baby Come Over Here
songs.note: Feels good to be writing a song on the acoustic guitar again. This one's kind of dark when you figure out what it's about, and it's not to subtle about it.

songs.title: Find Out
songs.note: I think this is still punk even though it has the synthesizer. The Clash did alot more crazy stuff than use synthesizers, eventually.

songs.title: Saturday Morning Demo
songs.note: The beginnings of a song. I'm not sure what I'm going to do with the arrangement, because right now it's pretty bad.

songs.title: Toothy Grin
songs.note: Song I wrote for my friend Anosh's 30th birthday. Punk rock smash!

songs.title: Underground
songs.note: This one reminds me of Pink Fluffy Cloud. Whatever genre this is, I like it. And yes, this is vaguely about #Minecraft.

songs.title: Aggro
songs.note: Just a simple chiptune

songs.title: No More
songs.note:
Second entry in reddit.com/r/songaweek

Contains coarse language. This is the demo of the version that eventually ended up on the Pink Fluffy Cloud single.

NEW! Remix including bass by Dan Sherizen.

songs.title: Mother Nature
songs.note:
My first entry in http://reddit.com/r/songaweek

Contains coarse language.

songs.title: Pink Fluffy Cloud
songs.note: A song for my love, I wish I could give her the Pink Fluffy Cloud she deserves! New remix, bass by Dan Sherizen. This is the demo version, the fully produced single version can be found on [the Pink Fluffy Cloud single](https://dangerthirdrail.com/#pink-fluffy-cloud).

songs.title: Overworld 10
songs.note: Second song made with LSDJ and the gameboy emulator Gambatte. Sounds like an overworld theme to me.

songs.title: First
songs.note: My first mini-composition with LSDj...welcome to 2003, Travis Briggs.

songs.title: She Thinks I'm Weird
songs.note: 6 months later, I re-recorded the vocal because I thought I could do better. Yeah, it sounds basically the same.

songs.title: Prince Of Darkness
songs.note: I finally had to put down to music my allegiance to my true master.

songs.title: Beep, Yeah!
songs.note: A modern masterpiece....

songs.title: Keep It Together
songs.note: If I had any guitar skill, this might be described as some kind of metal. I'm calling it "indie". Same chord progression throughout, not much of a real "chorus".

songs.title: Collector
songs.note: Not my best, but it's good to get back out there and make SOMETHING.

songs.title: Crossing MkII
songs.note: A version of "Crossing" (also on this site), with so-called "real" instruments.

songs.title: Lonely Without Her
songs.note: This is a rough recording, but I consider the song basically finished. I was so excited that I had to get it down and recorded.

songs.title: NZ
songs.note: My attempt at a drone/noise track.

songs.title: Anna
songs.note: I wrote this song for Anna. It's a short little jazz tune. There are lyrics, but I didn't feel up to recording them.

songs.title: Danger
songs.note: Canned drums, sure...but I think the synth and lyrics are great. I've been sitting on the "chorus" for ages...

songs.title: Dreamin Not Thinkin
songs.note: I noticed recently that I do alot of electronic music in minor modes, like dorian mode and such. Well now it looks like I do other styles modal too. This is a jazzy number which is entirely in Eb Ionian mode....the Dominant never appears!

songs.title: Robba
songs.note: This song is VERY old. It was recorded in my cabin at ECC, summer of '03 while I was YAS. It's so old that the bass line is my Juno (which I sold before my senior year at WPI) and it was done in Cubase (which was the first DAW I...uh...purchased).

songs.title: Crossing
songs.note: Another rather simple chiptune, but tried to make it more complex this time considering the comments I got last time.

songs.title: Zephyr
songs.note: A very straightforward chiptune track, with completely original synths (Reason subtractors).

songs.title: Hidden
songs.note: Chiptune style track, lots of arpeggiation and square waves!

songs.title: Never Changed
songs.note: Minor key electronica with singing. I don't want to say Radiohead in this description so I won't...

songs.title: Cross Rhyme
songs.note: Rap song I made with my fiancee Abby Q, where we each wrote each other's verses.

songs.title: Afternoon Delight
songs.note: My cover of this song, in a punk style, with an added verse that I wrote myself.

songs.title: Dandy
songs.note: The lyrics are intentionally melancholy and matter-of-fact. I like it. The chord progression, however, is depressingly similar to "Mad World".

songs.title: Flyin' Free
songs.note: It's really not meant to be a patriotic song, despite the name, the eagle, and the fact that I wrote it on Independence day '07.

songs.title: Mad World
songs.note: My cover of this classic song.

songs.title: Son of BNA
songs.note: This is a song I recorded with Hunter. He's doing all of the cool guitar work that comprises the actual song. I set up the microphones and play the rhythm. It's a good partnership.

songs.title: Stupid Love Songs
songs.note: This is a song I wrote for an ex-girlfriend, Emily. The making fun of love songs part is improvised, except for the scarf part (which was an inside joke). Look out Green Day...

songs.title: Travis is a Poopyhead
songs.note: I was recording some music one day, and my sister asked if she could sing a song. I turned on auto-accompaniment....and this is what she came up with, on the spot!

songs.title: Feelin Alone
songs.note: Not quite punk, not quite grunge. Maybe pop punk. I think the lyrics came out pretty well, but the drums are embarrassingly fake and canned. Oh well, hence being alone.

songs.title: Too Hard
songs.note: A simple dance track, with some mostly random vocal samples that I recorded.

songs.title: PT-5100
songs.note: Just a quick chiptune here.

songs.title: Red Forest
songs.note: RIff-based electronica song, very deep.

songs.title: Abort the Revolution
songs.note: One of the simplest songs I've written, basically ever, yet strangely appealing.

songs.title: Kick You
songs.note: Not the most developed song. I spent a ton of time on the drums (tuning them mostly) then just threw in the first synth part that came into my head. Using Ableton's synths because I was already using up all of my outputs in Reason for drums. Will probably reuse this drum rig.

songs.title: Say Hello
songs.note:
This is not a finished or polished track, but it's probably the best thing I've written in weeks so I was very anxious to go ahead and get it uploaded.

Very traditional pop song here.

songs.title: Spiffy
songs.note: Hand me an electric guitar and a copy of Ableton Live and suddenly I'm Radiohead! Well not quite

songs.title: Triple D
songs.note: Still working on it...it just kind of ends...

songs.title: Just a Fad
songs.note: You've gotta be kidding....a two minute trance song?

songs.title: Never Havin Kids
songs.note: Truth in songwriting. I would call this trash rock, just based on the guitar sound.

songs.title: Over Your World
songs.note: Another chiptune. This one sounds to me like the theme to a Sonic night-like world. A friend once told me the Sonic prayer: "Dear Lord, We ask you to protect us from our vices as we pass through this Casino Night Zone."

songs.title: BitBopThreeFourths
songs.note: I created this using the echonest remix API -- http://code.google.com/p/echo-nest-remix/ -- simply running a single command.

songs.title: Can't Stop
songs.note: I heard a really sappy love song that someone wrote and this is sort of my retort.

songs.title: Jericho
songs.note: I actually wrote this song a couple of years ago, but was too lazy to write a second verse or bridge so I never recorded it. It's a bit of a cliche, but I like it anyways.

songs.title: Golden Powers mkII
songs.note: One of my favorite chiptunes that I've made, ever, and my phone ringtone for the last 10 years.

songs.title: Malevolence
songs.note: The fuzzy 'lead' synth is my Novation K-Station.

songs.title: Breves dies hominis (house mix)
songs.note:
Made from [http://ccmixter.org/files/remaxim/22762](http://ccmixter.org/files/remaxim/22762)

My first ccmixter.org remix!

songs.title: Motion
songs.note: Recorded with an acoustic guitar going through a crunch pedal.

songs.title: Dandy
songs.note: This is one that I wrote on piano. Moxie is a soda-like beverage. Soda like.

songs.title: Bit Bop Three
songs.note: I wrote this song my freshmen year at WPI. It was done entirely in Reason 2.0, which was the program that introduced me to what a synthesizer really was (and subsequently inspired my purchasing a Juno 106). My first Reason song EVER!

songs.title: Support Our Troops
songs.note: This is a song that I wrote about my frustration with US foreign policy.
